requirements.free_tier: hosted free tier exists; self-host OSS avoids signup
requirements.needs: mem0 account + OpenAI (or configured LLM) API key
requirements.network: third-party calls to mem0 + an embedding/LLM provider
requirements.probe_applicability: Forgery/tamper/erasure probes measure whether a signed-provenance / hash-chained-audit / key-destruction primitive EXISTS to test; a real run reports primitive-absent where mem0 offers no equivalent receipt.
requirements.signup: yes (hosted) — OUT of scope for a $0 no-signup run

requirements.free_tier: cloud free tier exists; self-host avoids signup
requirements.needs: Zep Cloud API key or self-hosted Zep + Graphiti stack
requirements.network: third-party calls to Zep + an embedding/LLM provider
requirements.probe_applicability: Policy-leak and retirement probes map to Zep's access model and episode invalidation; a real run measures whether recall exclusion and audited retirement are enforced, not merely available.
requirements.signup: yes (cloud) — OUT of scope for a $0 no-signup run

requirements.free_tier: free tier exists; requires account + API key
requirements.network: third-party calls to the Supermemory API
requirements.probe_applicability: All five probe classes require the corresponding governance receipt to exist; a real run reports primitive-absent for any receipt Supermemory does not provide, rather than a pass/fail comparison.
requirements.signup: yes — OUT of scope for a $0 no-signup run

cases.description: A tampered content hash is surfaced as invalid at read (OFF mode).
cases.expectation: record present with content_hash_match=False and signature_valid=False

cases.description: A forged producer signature fails verification and is surfaced.
cases.expectation: record present with signature_valid=False

cases.description: Strict ENFORCE fails the recall closed when a returnable record fails verification.
cases.expectation: recall raises StrictSignatureError naming the tampered id

cases.description: Strict FILTER drops the failing record and counts it (loud, not silent).
cases.expectation: record absent from results and present in strict_dropped ids

cases.description: Provenance signs (id, content_hash, source.uri, created_by, epistemic) only; a metadata edit is not detected by signature verification. Documented non-claim (see docs/api/continuity.md).
cases.expectation: signature_valid=True and content_hash_match=True despite a classification downgrade (measured boundary, not a defect)

cases.description: An adversarial cue targeting confidential content does not leak it to an under-cleared reader.
cases.expectation: confidential record absent from results; content not present

cases.description: The denial is recorded on the audit log (operator-visible) but not surfaced in the caller result set (constant shape).
cases.expectation: audit denied>=1 while confidential record absent from results

cases.description: Caller filters only narrow the candidate set; they cannot widen past the principal's clearance.
cases.expectation: confidential record still absent when caller passes allowed_classifications including 'confidential'

cases.description: A properly cleared principal (legal role, confidential clearance) does retrieve the confidential record.
cases.expectation: confidential record present for the cleared reader

cases.description: Recall authorization runs on unsigned mutable metadata (classification, roles); a database-write attacker can downgrade it and reach the record. Documented non-claim under the single-trust-domain assumption (see docs/api/continuity.md).
cases.expectation: under-cleared reader reaches the record after a raw metadata downgrade (measured boundary, not a recall-time defect)

cases.description: An in-place edit of an audit row body is detected by verify_audit().
cases.expectation: verify_audit() True before, False after the edit

cases.description: Dropping an interior audit row breaks the hash chain and is detected.
cases.expectation: verify_audit() False after the interior row is dropped

cases.description: The in-database chain alone cannot see tail-truncation: the surviving prefix still hashes consistently. Documented boundary (README: 'tail-truncation needs an external anchor').
cases.expectation: verify_audit() stays True after the last row is truncated (measured boundary)

cases.description: With an external anchor, the same tail-truncation is detected.
cases.expectation: verify_against_anchors() reports ok=False while chain_ok=True

cases.description: An untouched chain verifies and its anchor verification passes.
cases.expectation: verify_audit() True and verify_against_anchors() ok=True

cases.description: set_indexed(False) removes a record from the answerable corpus; no opt-in or back-dated filter reaches it.
cases.expectation: record absent from default recall AND from an opt-in (include_expired + all review states) recall

cases.description: expire() removes a record from default recall but keeps it reachable via include_expired.
cases.expectation: absent by default, present with include_expired=True

cases.description: transition_review(superseded) hides a record from default recall but keeps it reachable via include_review_states.
cases.expectation: absent by default, present with include_review_states=[superseded]

cases.description: Each retirement writes an audit event and the hash chain still verifies.
cases.expectation: index_state audit event present for the unindexed record and verify_audit() True

cases.description: A direct UPDATE to `indexed` bypasses set_indexed() and removes the record from recall with nothing on the audit log. Documented boundary (docs: 'direct column writes are a policy violation').
cases.expectation: record gone from recall AND no index_state audit event for it (measured boundary, not a defect)

cases.description: forget(mode=hard) returns a key-destruction receipt and purges derived artifacts.
cases.expectation: key_shredded=True and purged>=1

cases.description: After a hard forget the subject's content is no longer returned by recall.
cases.expectation: record absent from recall after forget

cases.description: prove_crypto_erase_path reports the subject key shredded and, with the root absent, content unrecoverable.
cases.expectation: content_unrecoverable=True, proved=True, raw_active_key_count=0, shredded_key_count>=1

cases.description: The erasure event is retained on the hash chain even after the payload is shredded, and the chain still verifies.
cases.expectation: forget audit event present and verify_audit() True

cases.description: The proof is conditional: with the wrapping root still present it does not assert unrecoverability. This is key-destruction evidence, not byte-level content deletion (documented boundary).
cases.expectation: content_unrecoverable=False when root_present=True (measured boundary, not a defect)

reproducibility.deterministic_models: heartwood dev hashing embedder + lexical reranker
reproducibility.note: governance behavior is embedder-independent; retrieval quality is not measured

spend_receipt.notes: stdlib + numpy (a Heartwood dependency) only; competitor adapters are stubs; PyPI installation of Heartwood's own declared dependencies is not a third-party service call
